Let's start planning for one dot x. Consider all the planning documents. Update the @docs/progress.md  with weak level task granularity. Let's create new milestones in GitHub. Also, try to utilize the GitHub project `DataQ roadmap`. 
Few things to consider - The Snowflake subscription is going to end by this week. The Azure subscription is for another three weeks. We have to utilize them to the fullest before the subscription ends.

I'll start by reading the planning documents — primarily [context/post-v1-roadmap.md](context/post-v1-roadmap.md) — then draft the week-level cycle plan factoring in the Snowflake (this week) and Azure (~3 weeks) subscription deadlines.

1	# Post-v1 roadmap — the single home for deferred work
2	
3	> **What this is:** the canonical index of everything deliberately deferred past DataQ v1 —
4	> design themes (with their detailed design docs) **and** the concrete issue backlog. Every
5	> issue on the GitHub **`Backlog (post-v1 / testing)`** milestone is, by definition, post-v1
6	> and is mirrored here under the theme it belongs to.
7	>
8	> **What this is not:** a commitment or a schedule. v1 is the 8-week product (see
9	> [DataQ_platform_roadmap.md](DataQ_platform_roadmap.md) +
10	> [progress-v1.md](../docs/progress-v1.md), the archived v1 ledger). These are the things we consciously chose *not* to build for v1,
11	> captured so the intent isn't lost.
12	>
13	> **Source of truth for status:** the GitHub milestone. This doc is the human-readable map;
14	> when an issue closes, it closes on GitHub — don't hand-maintain `[x]` here.
15	>
16	> **Why it lives in `context/`:** alongside [DataQ_platform_roadmap.md](DataQ_platform_roadmap.md),
17	> this is the **input for a post-v1 week-wise task generator** — once v1 ships, this themed backlog
18	> + the design docs feed the planner that produces the v1.x / v2.x weekly task breakdown. Keep it
19	> generator-friendly: themes with intent, issue refs, and pointers to the detailed design.
20	
21	**Detailed design docs** (the "why" + the shape):
22	- [post-v1-admin-ui-notes.md](../docs/post-v1-admin-ui-notes.md) — admin / access model / UI & IA
23	- [post-v1-dq-intelligence-notes.md](../docs/post-v1-dq-intelligence-notes.md) — expectation expansion, LLM-assisted authoring, marketplace
24	- [compliance-posture.md](../docs/compliance-posture.md) — GDPR / CCPA-CPRA / HIPAA technical controls + gap list
25	
26	**The big picture:** v1 ships the DQ loop (checks → results → trends → freshness/volume monitors
27	→ alerts → MCP) end-to-end. Post-v1 layers *intelligence* (more expectations, LLM authoring,
28	the remaining monitor kinds), *governance* (admin console, compliance controls), and *scale*
29	(performance hardening, second-impl seams) on top of it.

33	## v1 maturity assessment — where the product honestly stands (2026-07-03)
34	
35	> A calibrated self-assessment recorded at Week-7 close so post-v1 prioritisation starts from
36	> reality, not from the changelog. Verdict: **strong engineering artifact, ~4/10 as a
37	> competitive DQ product today.** Confidence it does what it's configured to do: ~8/10
38	> (live-verified). Confidence a data team should pick it over Soda/Elementary/GX Cloud today:
39	> ~3/10. Confidence the architecture can close the gap: ~7/10 — the seams are the right ones.
40	> One-liner: v1 is a well-engineered, security-conscious **"GX-as-a-service with orchestration
41	> awareness"** — credible as a single-team internal tool; not yet in the conversation with
42	> commercial DQ platforms.
43	
44	**What's genuinely strong (defend without hesitation):**
45	- End-to-end **verified**, not claimed: 4 datasource run paths green against live infra;
46	  orchestration-triggered checks proven (ADF failure → visible in 4m14s; trigger-on-success
47	  firing off real pipeline runs). Trigger-on-pipeline-success is a real differentiator over
48	  cron-only checkers.
49	- The expensive-to-retrofit architecture is already right: monitor-kind seam (ADR 0012),
50	  adapter/runner registries (ADR 0011), `ResultPublisher`, provider-agnostic infra (ADR 0010).
51	- Security/PII posture well above v1 norm: logger-level + column-aware redaction, tested authz
52	  matrix, fail-closed MCP, incident-driven hardening. Alerting has dedup/snooze/severity routing.
53	- MCP integration is ahead of the market curve and tested end-to-end.
54	
55	**The honest gaps (each mapped to where it's tracked):**
56	
57	| # | Gap | Why it matters | Where tracked |
58	|---|---|---|---|
59	| G-a | **Monitors only what you remember to check** — hand-authored expectations; no anomaly baselines, no schema-drift, no auto-suggested checks. Category leaders' core pitch (Monte Carlo/Anomalo) is the inverse: automatic coverage. An unknown-unknown incident sails past DataQ. | Existential for "product"; the single biggest gap | **Theme 1** (remaining monitor kinds: `schema_drift`, `anomaly`) + **Theme 2** (auto-suggestion/LLM authoring) |
60	| G-b | **Scale unproven; structurally weak on 2 of 4 paths** — flat-file + UC runners load the whole file/table into worker pandas; largest table ever validated is a few thousand synthetic rows. No sampling/partition/incremental strategy for checks. (Snowflake pushes down via SQL — fine.) | 100M-row table = worker OOM; blocks any serious deployment | **Theme 7** (perf & scale — add: sampling/partition-aware/incremental check execution as a named workstream) |
61	| G-c | **Every "verified" is self-referential** — one operator, ~1 week live, against a harness built by the same author. Zero real-world data-pathology exposure at scale (schema churn, half-written files, encoding chaos in volume). | Unknown failure modes in the first real deployment | **Theme 10** (test-hardening) + first-real-workload milestone, post-v1 |
62	| G-d | **No incident workflow, no lineage, no ownership routing** — runs + alerts exist; "what broke downstream / who owns it / when was it resolved" doesn't. No data-access audit trail (the HIPAA gate). | This is what DQ products are bought for | **Theme 9** (results/reporting depth) + **Theme 4** / [#431](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/431) (audit trail); lineage/incident objects = new design doc needed; the incident-*narrative* half is design-captured as **Theme 2's agentic root-cause analysis** (2026-07-04); lineage may be *pulled/emitted* rather than built — **Theme 14** governance-catalog + OpenLineage capture (2026-07-04); incident objects anchor to the **asset entity** (Theme 3's Asset-first IA capture, phase 1 = the shared prerequisite) |
63	| G-e | **Single-tenant, config-allowlist admin, one validated IdP** — fine internally, not sellable. | Blocks multi-team/commercial use | **Theme 3** (admin/access) + ADR 0026 / [#461](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/461) (API keys) |
64	| G-f | **Ecosystem: 4 datasources** vs the 30–50 a category product ships; no dbt integration (seam reserved); can't check the Postgres it runs on. | Adoption ceiling | **Theme 8** (datasource depth; generic-RDBMS adapter is the cheap first win) |
65	| G-g | **Engine risk: GX Core pin** — the product's core capability rides a fast-moving third party with documented API drift; DQX swap-in shape exists for UC only. | Strategic dependency | **Theme 2**'s engine-abstraction watch item (below) — no issue until the churn trigger fires |
66	| G-h | **Harness Databricks = Free Edition, non-commercial-only** — the UC demo leg cannot legally back a commercial demo, while ADR 0013's ambition is commercial BYOL. **Decision recorded 2026-07-03 (go-live):** acceptable while the deployment is demo/eval; trigger stands — before any commercial demo/use → paid workspace. | Licence landmine on the demo path | ADR 0021/0013 context — recorded in the go-live checklist ([progress-v1.md](../docs/progress-v1.md)); re-trigger **before any commercial demo** |
67	| G-i | **Pre-marketplace teardown: the deployed app still carries the demo harness** — Flows A/B/C, the 5 harness connections (Snowflake/UC/ADLS/ADF/Airflow), demo suites/users, and the seeded-breach check are all ADR-0021 *test* fixtures, not product. Before any marketplace listing / distributable image / customer-facing deployment (ADR 0013): remove the harness flows + datasource connections + demo users from the reference deployment, and verify nothing in the shipped artifact references harness endpoints. (Noted 2026-07-03 with the G-h decision.) | A demo fixture shipping as if it were product surface — licence (G-h) + credibility + stale-credential risk | ADR 0013 pre-listing checklist item — this row is the note; fold into the marketplace workstream when Theme 2's marketplace work is picked up |
68	
69	**Prioritisation signal for the post-v1 planner:** G-a and G-b are the two that change what the
70	product *is* (coverage-without-authoring + scale-safe execution); G-d is what makes it *usable in
71	anger* (incidents/lineage); the rest are adoption/positioning. Recommended post-v1 opening
72	sequence: Theme 1 `schema_drift` + `anomaly` (rides the ADR 0012 seam, no schema rewrite) →
73	scale-aware execution (G-b, Theme 7) → incident/lineage design doc (G-d).

77	## Theme 1 — Monitor kinds (the remaining reserved kinds)
78

99	## Theme 2 — DQ-intelligence (expectation expansion, LLM authoring, marketplace)
100	
101	Full design: **[post-v1-dq-intelligence-notes.md](../docs/post-v1-dq-intelligence-notes.md)**. The enabler is
102	already true in v1 — no server-side expectation allowlist, so "add an expectation" is mostly a
103	frontend-catalog + config-validation problem. Four themes: (1) the 5 high-ROI GX built-ins,
104	(2) LLM custom-SQL generator, (3) LLM curated check-suggestions, (4a) curated server-served
105	catalog + allowlist. LLM integration = admin-configured, default-off, BYO-credential `LLMProvider`
106	seam (schema-only, PII-redacted context).
107	
108	| # | Title |
109	|---|---|
110	| [#124](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/124) | Add DQ-dimension classification to checks (Completeness / Uniqueness / Validity / …) |
111	| [#286](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/286) | Apache Iceberg v2 / v3 table-format support |
112	
113	*(The built-ins / LLM / marketplace work is design-captured but not yet filed as discrete issues —
114	file from the detail doc when picked up.)*
115	
116	**Engine-abstraction watch item (from maturity-assessment G-g):** the product's core capability
117	rides a pinned GX Core with documented API drift (CLAUDE.md §11); the DQX swap-in shape exists
118	for the UC runner only. If GX churn continues (or DQX/v1.1 lands), generalise that runner-level
119	engine seam beyond UC so the check engine is a pluggable impl, not a hard dependency. No issue
120	filed yet — file one when the trigger fires.
121	
122	### Agentic root-cause analysis (design-captured 2026-07-04 — the category-leading bet)
123	
124	**When a check fails, DataQ investigates — it doesn't just alert.** Every DQ product today stops
125	at detection ("row count dropped 40% 🔴") and a human spends the next two hours on why. DataQ's
126	moat for closing that loop already exists in the v1 schema: the `triggered_by`
127	`pipeline_runs` ↔ `runs` correlation (cron-only checkers structurally lack it), `metric_value` as
128	a SQL-aggregatable trend scalar (ADR 0012), the column profiler, and redacted failing samples
129	(#417). Two-layer, LLM-degradable design:
130	
131	1. **Deterministic evidence card (no LLM anywhere).** On a `fail`/`critical` result, a Celery
132	   task assembles the dossier from existing data: the upstream pipeline run (status + duration/
133	   delay vs. its own history), the check's `metric_value` trend ("sudden vs. slow drift"), a
134	   profile-diff of the failing batch vs. the last passing one (which segment broke), sibling
135	   checks on the same table, and downstream suites (blast radius). Ships as a structured card on
136	   the alert via the existing `ResultPublisher` seam. Most of the triage value lives here, with
137	   zero new dependencies — **build this first**.
138	2. **LLM narrative + ranked causal hypothesis (optional).** Turns the dossier into a
139	   three-sentence diagnosis. Rides the **same admin-configured, default-off, BYO-credential
140	   `LLMProvider` seam** as the authoring work above (one seam, two features; endpoint/key in the
141	   SecretStore like any connection; any OpenAI-compatible/Anthropic/Azure-OpenAI/local endpoint).
142	   Context is schema-only + column-aware-redacted samples — the model sees what a non-admin user
143	   would. **Fail-open:** no LLM connection configured → layer 1 still ships in full.
144	
145	**Zero-config interactive path:** the MCP server (Theme 13) exposes the same dossier as tools, so
146	a user's *own* Claude/Copilot is the reasoning engine and DataQ never holds an LLM key — the
147	push-narrative-into-the-alert version is the premium ergonomics, not a gate.
148	
149	This is the **incident-narrative half of gap G-d** done LLM-natively, and it compounds with
150	Theme 1 (anomaly baselines sharpen the dossier) and Theme 5 (the card enriches the alert
151	payload). No issues filed yet — file the two layers as separate issues when picked up (layer 1
152	has no LLM dependency and can ship alone).
153	
154	---
155	
156	## Theme 3 — Admin, access model & UI/IA
157	
158	Full design: **[post-v1-admin-ui-notes.md](../docs/post-v1-admin-ui-notes.md)**. v1 ships suite-level sharing
159	+ a read-only workspace-admin view (#289). A full RBAC console is gold-plating for a single-tenant
160	trusted-team tool; the market leaders lead with checks→results→trends→alerts, not user-lifecycle.
161	
162	| # | Title |
163	|---|---|
164	| [#411](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/411) | Workspace-admin: workspace-wide view on Dashboard + Results (both are owned-or-shared scoped today) |
165	| [#412](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/412) | Admin page is read-only — allow workspace-admin write actions (manage shares / suites) |
166	| [#461](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/461) | **DataQ-issued API keys / service tokens** (REST + MCP) — see below |
167	| _(no issue yet)_ | **Data-asset-centric view** — the biggest IA gap (added 2026-07-04): the UI is suite-centric but users think table-centric ("is `orders` healthy?"). A dataset page aggregating every check, result, freshness state, and trend across all suites targeting that table/file pattern. The natural UI anchor for Theme 14's lineage/governance pull and the Theme-2 RCA blast radius; category leaders are asset-first for this reason. **Phase 2 of the Asset-first IA capture below** — build the asset entity first. |
168	| _(no issue yet)_ | **Global search / command palette (⌘K)** — jump to any suite/check/connection/run by name. The list APIs exist; disproportionate daily-use payoff for a small build. |
169	| _(no issue yet)_ | **First-run onboarding + empty-state pass** — guided connect → suite → check → run path and designed empty states. Low value for the current solo deployment, first-touch-critical for any marketplace/BYOL evaluator (ADR 0013); cheap to retrofit now vs. embarrassing at listing time. |
170	| _(no issue yet)_ | **Bulk operations on checks** — multi-select enable/disable/severity/snooze; one-at-a-time editing doesn't survive 50-check suites. |
171	
172	**DataQ-issued API keys / service tokens (#461, ADR [0026](../docs/adr/0026-auth-api-keys-and-principal-seam.md) proposed).** Auth today is Azure-AD-only (delegated/SSO) for both REST and `/mcp` — the deepest remaining vendor lock-in (the `get_current_user` seam has one real impl; `users.aad_object_id` is Azure-shaped) and it blocks BYOL-on-AWS/GCP (ADR 0013) and headless/programmatic access (a long-lived scoped key beats a ~60-min refreshing token for CI / always-on MCP clients). The fix is a **second authenticator behind the same `get_current_user` seam** so the **REST API and MCP accept it identically** — never MCP-only — which also finally *exercises* the seam (ADR 0010). Phase it: **user-scoped PATs first** (inherit the owner's per-suite grants → zero new authz; optional read-only down-scope), defer standalone **service-account principals** (they force generalizing `aad_object_id` → a generic principal with pluggable identity bindings + non-user suite sharing). Credential bar: hashed-at-rest + show-once + prefix + expiry + revocation + audit, in a new `api_keys` table (not the retrievable-secret SecretStore), with key lifecycle tied to the owner so it can't outlive a deactivated account.
173	
174	### Accessibility & inclusive UI (design-captured 2026-07-04 — a genuine blind spot)
175	
176	Nothing in the repo mentions accessibility today — flagged in the 2026-07-04 UI review as the
177	gap most likely to convert from "nice" to **procurement checkbox** the moment a commercial buyer
178	with compliance requirements appears (it belongs next to Theme 4 in the "sellability" bucket).
179	Target: **WCAG 2.1 AA**, phased so it's cheap early instead of a retrofit crisis:
180	
181	1. **Automated floor first** — wire `axe-core` into the existing Playwright E2E lane (25 specs
182	   already run in CI) + `vitest-axe` for component tests; fail CI on new serious/critical
183	   violations. This is the ratchet that stops regression before any manual work starts.
184	2. **Non-color severity cues** — severity is communicated almost entirely by color today
185	   (red/orange/green tags, chart series). Add icons/shapes/text alongside color and adopt a
186	   colorblind-safe palette (also fixes the recharts dashboards, where color is the *only*
187	   encoding). Highest user-impact single item — ~8% of male users can't reliably read the
188	   current severity language.
189	3. **Keyboard & focus audit** — full keyboard traversal of the 13-screen set; focus-trap +
190	   restore on the surviving drawers/modals (Share, version history, run progress, import);
191	   visible focus states; skip-to-content. antd gives a decent baseline; the custom
192	   drawer/table/editor compositions are where it breaks.
193	4. **Screen-reader semantics** — landmarks, table headers/captions on the results/runs tables,
194	   aria-live for async run-progress updates, and **text alternatives for every chart**
195	   (the dashboards are pure-visual today; a data-table fallback per chart covers both a11y and
196	   the PDF-export path, #345).
197	5. **Related, decide-later:** reduced-motion support (cheap, ride item 2), dark mode (already
198	   Theme 12), i18n/locale externalization (defer until a non-English prospect exists — but stop
199	   hard-coding user-facing strings in new code *now*, which costs nothing).
200	
201	No issues filed yet — file items 1–4 as separate issues when picked up; item 1 can land
202	independently as a Theme-10-style CI ratchet.
203	
204	### Asset-first IA (design-captured 2026-07-04 — the lineage-era navigation model)
205	
206	**When lineage lands (Theme 14), invert the navigation from suite-first to asset-first — but
207	split the noun from the verb rather than replacing one with the other.** Assets become what
208	users *browse and reason about* (health, lineage, incidents, blast radius); suites remain how
209	checks *execute* (batching, scheduling, trigger bindings, notifications, sharing). Making assets
210	primary for *everything* would fight three structural facts: authz is suite-scoped (ADR 0027)
211	and re-keying permissions to assets is a painful migration for little gain; scheduling /
212	triggers / notifications are genuinely batch-level operational concepts; and GX itself is
213	suite-shaped, so the engine grain stays suites regardless. The products that do this well (dbt
214	models-vs-jobs, Monte Carlo assets-vs-monitors) all run this two-axis model.
215	
216	**The enabling primitive — build once, four features consume it:** a first-class **asset
217	entity**. Today "the table" exists only implicitly inside `Suite.target` and check configs.
218	Lineage needs asset *nodes*; the asset page needs asset *identity*; G-d incidents need something
219	to *attach to*; governance-catalog sync (Theme 14) needs an entity to *map to*. One migration
220	serves all four — schedule it before any of them.
221	
222	**Asset identity = the OpenLineage dataset naming spec (namespace + name), adopted as the
223	canonical key from day one.** It answers the hardest resolution problems (the same logical table
224	via DEV vs QA connections, flat-file patterns as assets, UC three-level names) with a
225	vendor-neutral convention, and makes the Theme-14 OpenLineage emission/pull interop automatic
226	instead of a mapping layer.
227	
228	**Phases (each shippable alone):**
229	1. **Asset entity + resolution** — `assets` table keyed by OpenLineage naming + the mapping of
230	   checks/runs to asset IDs. The migration everything else rides.
231	2. **Asset view** (the row above) as a read-only aggregation. Authz subtlety: an asset page
232	   aggregates *across* suites, so it must filter to the caller's suite grants and never leak
233	   the existence of unshared checks — the same 404-no-leak discipline verified live at go-live.
234	3. **Lineage attach** — asset pages grow upstream/downstream + blast radius; the Theme-2 RCA
235	   dossier and G-d incident objects anchor to assets.
236	4. **Navigation inversion** — sidebar leads with Assets, dashboard health aggregates per asset
237	   (more meaningful to data consumers than per-suite), suites demote to an "execution groups"
238	   surface; suite CRUD and deep links keep working untouched. This is the step where DataQ
239	   stops feeling like "GX-as-a-service" and starts feeling like a data-observability product —
240	   the G-a/G-d repositioning the maturity assessment calls for.
241	
242	No issues filed yet — phase 1 files first when picked up (it's the dependency of the other
243	three and of Theme 14's governance sync).
244	
245	---
246	
247	## Theme 4 — Compliance (GDPR / CCPA-CPRA / HIPAA)
248	
249	Full design: **[compliance-posture.md](../docs/compliance-posture.md)**. v1 is privacy-by-design (logger PII
250	redaction, default-redact samples, retention purge, SecretStore, suite-scoped authz, BYOL
251	controller/processor split). These close the gaps for a credible v2.x "processor-grade controls" claim.
252	
253	| # | Gap | Regime hook |
254	|---|---|---|
255	| [#431](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/431) | 🔴 **G1** data-access audit trail (who read which result/sample) | HIPAA §164.312(b) — the HIPAA gate |
256	| [#432](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/432) | 🟠 **G2** data-subject-rights machinery (erasure / access / portability) | GDPR Art 15/17/20, CCPA |
257	| [#433](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/433) | 🟠 **G3** authoritative PII/PHI classification (warehouse tags over name heuristic) | — |
258	| [#434](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/434) | 🟠 **G4** region/residency assertion + enforcement | GDPR Ch. V; LLM transfer vector |
259	| [#435](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/435) | 🟡 **G5** assert encryption-at-rest in IaC + offer CMK | — |
260	
261	### Privacy pack (design-captured 2026-07-04)
262	
263	Builds on **G3/#433** (authoritative warehouse-tag classification) and **absorbs the
264	classification remainder** left open when #415 closed with #417's column-aware redaction —
265	the new pieces, no issues filed yet:
266	
267	- **Profiler-side PII auto-detection** as an additional classification *source*: the profiler
268	  already visits every column; lightweight format/regex detectors (no LLM) persist a per-column
269	  classification tag, and redaction reads **tags** instead of name heuristics. Classification
270	  sources compose: warehouse tags (#433) > governance-catalog pull (Theme 14) > profiler
271	  heuristics, most-authoritative wins.
272	- **`pii_drift` monitor kind** — "a column that looks like email addresses just appeared in a
273	  table not classified as containing PII." Rides the ADR 0012 seam next to Theme 1's
274	  `schema_drift`; the alert privacy teams actually fear, and one mainstream DQ tools don't fire.
275	- **Zero-sample "privacy mode"** — a deployment-level switch where failing-row samples are
276	  never persisted (aggregates + `metric_value` + unexpected-counts only). Mostly a write-path
277	  gate; turns the existing redaction stack into a tiered posture: full → column-aware
278	  redacted (#417) → zero-sample. The first question HIPAA-tier / EU deployments ask.
279	
280	---
281	
282	## Theme 5 — Alerting depth (beyond the v1 Teams/Slack/email seam)
283	
284	v1 ships the `ResultPublisher` seam with Teams + Slack + email, severity routing, dedup, snooze.
285	These enrich and de-risk it:
286	
287	| # | Title |
288	|---|---|
289	| [#416](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/416) | Enrich Slack/email alerts: deep link to run, per-check expected-vs-observed, actionable sample, run metadata |

348	with — the Theme-1 opening sequence; different layer of the stack): PG adapter → MSSQL/Fabric
349	adapter → OneLake spike; then the cloud-triad completion (BigQuery → Redshift → GCS → MySQL),
350	with Trino/Athena slotted whenever federation demand shows up (it can jump the queue — it's the
351	G-f multiplier).

353	**Decided-against (2026-07-03): no native Cosmos DB adapter.** Cosmos has no SQL dialect
354	SQLAlchemy/GX can drive, and the DataFrame-reader alternative (Cosmos SDK → pandas, the UC-runner
355	shape) has RU-cost + scale problems that make it a bad default. Coverage path of record: **via its
356	Fabric mirror** → the MSSQL adapter reads the mirror's SQL analytics endpoint. Revisit a native
357	adapter only on real demand.
358	
359	**Demand-driven boundary (recorded 2026-07-04) — major but deliberately not default:**
360	Oracle / Teradata / SAP HANA (real enterprise demand, heavy drivers + licensing — build on a
361	concrete prospect, not speculatively); **MongoDB** (same no-SQL-dialect logic as Cosmos, same
362	routing answer: via Trino's connector or a mirror); **Kafka / streaming** (not batch — that's the
363	DQX v1.1 lane, ADR [0003](../docs/adr/0003-gx-only-for-v1.md)); **Delta / Iceberg table formats

484	## Theme 14 — Ecosystem & vendor-neutral portability (added 2026-07-04)
485	
486	Meet users in the tools they already run — incident/ITSM, test management, data governance,
487	GitOps — and keep every integration behind a seam with **open standards first** (ADR 0010/0013:
488	no tool becomes architecture; each vendor is one impl). All design-captured, no issues filed yet.
489	
490	### Checks-as-code: `dataq.yaml` + a CI gate
491	
492	Formalize the existing export/import format into a **versioned declarative suite format** plus a
493	CLI / GitHub Action: `dataq validate` in a data-pipeline repo's CI, `dataq apply` to sync,
494	**drift detection** between repo and workspace. The market leaders (Soda, GX Cloud) lead with
495	this workflow; DataQ's angle is that the format **round-trips with a full UI and the
496	suite-scoped authz model** — checks-as-code tools mostly have no real UI, UI tools no real
497	GitOps. Hard dependency: **ADR 0026 / #461** (PATs) — this is its killer use case, and the
498	argument for scheduling PATs early in the cycle.
499	
500	### Incident / ITSM — tier 2, bidirectional
501	
502	Tier 1 (create-only PagerDuty / Opsgenie / ServiceNow / Jira via the generic signed-webhook
503	publisher + payload templates) lives in **Theme 5** and needs zero vendor code. Tier 2 is the
504	ITSM-grade half: DataQ incident objects (the G-d design) sync *both ways* — ack/resolve in
505	PagerDuty/ServiceNow reflects in DataQ, Jira issue links attach to results — and the **Theme 2
506	agentic-RCA evidence card is the incident payload**, so the ticket arrives with the diagnosis
507	pre-attached. Per-vendor impls behind one `IncidentProvider`-shaped seam, only after tier 1
508	proves demand.
509	
510	### Test-management publishing (TestRail, Xray, Zephyr)
511	
512	DQ artifacts map cleanly: suites → test plans, checks → cases, runs → test runs with results.
513	A result-*exporter* seam (a `ResultPublisher` sibling — per-run push, not per-alert) publishes
514	run outcomes into the test-management tool of record; pairs with Theme 9's PDF export (#345)
515	as the "reporting into someone else's system of record" family.
516	
517	### Data-governance catalogs (+ OpenLineage)
518	
519	Two directions, one seam:
520	
521	- **Pull** — consume the catalog's glossary/PII classifications as an authoritative
522	  classification source (feeds Theme 4's privacy pack + #433, ranked above profiler
523	  heuristics), and **pull lineage** to power the RCA blast-radius (Theme 2) and gap G-d
524	  instead of building a lineage graph ourselves.
525	- **Push** — publish DQ results as **quality facets/assertions on catalog entities**, so data
526	  consumers see check status where they shop for data. DataHub and OpenMetadata both have
527	  first-class assertion/data-quality APIs — natural open-source reference impls; Microsoft
528	  Purview covers the Azure story; Collibra / Atlan / Alation are commercial targets behind the
529	  same seam.
530	- **Open standard first: emit OpenLineage events** from runs — one vendor-neutral event stream
531	  consumed by Marquez, DataHub, Purview, Atlan and the orchestrators' own lineage backends;
532	  cheaper and more neutral than any point integration, and the likely first slice.
533	
534	**Shared primitive:** this whole section keys on the **asset entity** (Theme 3's Asset-first IA
535	capture, phase 1) with **OpenLineage dataset naming as the canonical identity** — build that
536	first; catalog mapping, lineage nodes, and quality-facet publishing all resolve through it.
537	
538	### Observability & deploy portability
539	
540	Completes the last half-open ADR 0010 seam (tracked issues previously unmapped in this doc):

545	| _(no issue yet)_ | **Generic OTLP exporter endpoint** (`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`) — App Insights becomes one backend among any OTLP consumer (Grafana/Tempo, Datadog, Jaeger); the observability twin of the `DATAQ_AUTH_*` generic-OIDC cutover (ADR 0028), and a BYOL/marketplace prerequisite in spirit (ADR 0013) |
546	| [#505](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/505) | AWS + GCP deploy IaC behind the provider-agnostic seams (ADR 0028 follow-up) |

- **Datasource coverage:** SQL paths (information_schema / DESCRIBE) + flat-file (header/dtypes via the existing profiler machinery) + UC. All testable cloud-independently (local PG-dogfood optional, flat-file local, Databricks Free).
- **Authoring UI:** check-editor kind variant like #437's freshness/volume panels; dry-run support.
- **Monitors engine follow-ups #427/#428/#429 land the same week** — touching the same code, do them first or fold them in as prep commits (separate PRs per working agreements).

- Pairs with the per-check metric-trend view (#594) — same week so the trend chart can draw the learned band, which is also how you visually debug the model.

- **Guardrail:** a size probe + configurable hard cap so the worker refuses-with-\`error\` instead of OOMing.
- **Perf batch same week:** #327 (profiler N+1) + #323 (retention-sweep index/batch) — same theme, same harness.
- Testable cloud-independently: local files at parameterized volume (HARNESS_TODO §6 generator) + Databricks Free for UC.

#!/bin/bash
# Add v1.1 cycle issues to the DataQ Roadmap project (number 1), set "v1.1 week" + Status=Todo.
set -euo pipefail

OWNER=TheurgicDuke771
REDACTED
REDACTED
REDACTED
TODO_OPT=f75ad846

# week-option-id issue-number pairs
declare -a ITEMS=(
  "290ef67d 587" "290ef67d 588" "290ef67d 194" "290ef67d 195" "290ef67d 583" "290ef67d 532"
  "b7d6d02c 589" "b7d6d02c 524" "b7d6d02c 372" "b7d6d02c 461" "b7d6d02c 584"
  "704c2172 590" "704c2172 591" "704c2172 492"
  "0f8ce3e2 592" "0f8ce3e2 427" "0f8ce3e2 428" "0f8ce3e2 429"
  "37c1a30f 593" "37c1a30f 594" "37c1a30f 568"
  "12dd9999 595" "12dd9999 596" "12dd9999 327" "12dd9999 323" "12dd9999 563" "12dd9999 573"
)

for entry in "${ITEMS[@]}"; do
  opt=${entry% *}
  num=${entry#* }
  item_id=$(gh project item-add 1 --owner "$OWNER" \
    --url "https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/$num" --format json -q '.id')
  gh project item-edit --id "$item_id" --project-id "$PROJECT_ID" \
    --field-id "$WEEK_FIELD" --single-select-option-id "$opt" >/dev/null
  gh project item-edit --id "$item_id" --project-id "$PROJECT_ID" \
    --field-id "$STATUS_FIELD" --single-select-option-id "$TODO_OPT" >/dev/null
  echo "#$num -> $opt (Todo)"
done
